items.title: Celine Cremer searchers turn attention to missing man Peter Willoughby

children.content: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.

image.altText: A man with grey hair and glasses

items.title: 'Deeply disappointed': Premier silent on leaked email complaint

children.content: Ruth Forrest says she is "deeply disappointed" about a lack of government response over a leaked email and is considering referring a complaint to WorkSafe Tasmania and parliament's Privileges Committee.

image.altText: Woman speaking at lectern

items.title: Once a dumping ground, Aboriginal ownership is restoring land

image.altText: A bushland area with large ferns and tall trees.

children.content: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.

items.title: TassieCast 🔊 Housing failures, why are we still falling short?

children.content: One in three Tassie households are struggling with rental affordability ... if you can find somewhere to rent, that is.

gallery.altText: New houses in a sub division in Tasmania.

thumbnail.altText: New houses in a sub division in Tasmania.

items.title: Farmers desperate for nitrogen 'drug' search for substitutes

image.altText: Man stands for portrait among apple orchard

children.content: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

items.title: Greens push for minister's resignation after legal fee revelation

children.content: Madeleine Ogilvie is being accused of deliberately misleading parliament, after she revealed her involvement in a Supreme Court matter, months after saying she was not connected to any legal action.

image.altText: A woman sits in a green chair in parliament

items.title: Hopes inquest into Hillcrest jumping castle tragedy will begin in September

children.content: A coronial inquest into the deaths of six children at a Tasmanian primary school could begin in September, nearly five years after the tragedy occurred.

image.altText: Composite image of six victims of jumping castle tragedy with school signage as background

items.title: Tasmania introduces 'nation-first' levy on family violence offenders

children.content: New laws designed to strengthen family violence protections in Tasmania include an extension to the statutory limitation period for coercive control offences, and a "nation-leading" levy which would be paid by offenders.

items.title: 'Horrible' heatwave has Brits wondering if their country is built for heat

children.content: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

gallery.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Trump says Murdoch indicated he would 'handle' newspaper's Epstein story

children.content: In refiling his lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal, the US president alleges that Rupert Murdoch indicated that a story about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein would not be published.

image.altText: Rupert Murdoch and Donald Trump on a golf course.

items.title: French Open favourite loses to 56th-ranked amid dizzy spell

children.content: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggled with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

image.altText: Jannik Sinner sits in discomfort during a change of ends at the French Open.

items.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now

children.content: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.

image.altText: An older man in a suit with glasses leans in as he speaks into a microphone.

items.title: AFP defends public arrest of Ben Roberts-Smith

children.content: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a navy police officer uniform.

items.title: What does it mean to be the best jack of all trades, but master of none?

children.content: Elite athletes are constantly striving for perfection — an extra millisecond shaved off their time or another centimetre on their distance — but for heptathletes like Tori West, the equation is a little bit harder.

gallery.altText: An image of Tori West running surrounding by six pictures of her in heptathlon events.

thumbnail.altText: An image of Tori West running surrounding by six pictures of her in heptathlon events.

items.title: Austrian man jailed for Taylor Swift concert terror plot

children.content: A man who plotted but ultimately failed to launch a terrorist attack at Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, forcing the pop star to cancel three performances, is sentenced to 15 years behind bars.

image.altText: Taylor Swift in an orange bejewelled leotard on stage.

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly-skilled white collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: Friday news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback

children.content: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

gallery.altText: A close up image of a jar full of money, with a 'swear jar' label on the front.

thumbnail.altText: A close up image of a jar full of money, with a 'swear jar' label on the front.

items.title: The Aussie rocker who mastered the video game needle drop

children.content: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

image.altText: a man in a checked shirt and black and white sunglasses leans against a blue VW beetle.

items.title: Growing garlic is one of life's slower lessons

children.content: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

image.altText: Koren holds garlic bulbs in a basket, she's smiling.

avatar.alt: A woman sits in her front garden, which she replanted with native plants to attract local butterflies and insects.

items.title: Man devastated to lose home deposit in 'eco village' development

children.content: It was lauded as a solution to the housing crisis, but those involved say plans to build an "eco village" in northern New South Wales have gone awry.

gallery.altText: Serge Killingbeck wears a hat with his arms crossed behind a garden.

thumbnail.altText: Serge Killingbeck wears a hat with his arms crossed behind a garden.

avatar.alt: Photo of ABC Newcastle investigative reporter Alex Turner-Cohen, smiling and wearing a white shirt, arms folded

items.title: Netanyahu directs Israel's military to take over 70 per cent of Gaza

children.content: Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of Gaza, now bombarded to ruins by Israel's two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas attack on southern Israel.

image.altText: A little boy looks through tent sheets.

items.title: Major wine retailer puts vineyards up for sale amid industry-wide squeeze

children.content: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

image.altText: Wine bottles on a bottle shop shelf.

items.title: Insect repellent may not always keep mozzies away, study suggests

children.content: Scientists have found that mosquitoes in the lab can be trained to connect the smell of a widely used repellent with food, but repellents are still the best way to protect yourself.

gallery.altText: Close up photo of a mosquito on a green leaf.

thumbnail.altText: Close up photo of a mosquito on a green leaf.

items.title: Saints' tough loss made worse as midfielder injures Achilles

children.content: Hawthorn consolidates its top four spot with a big win over St Kilda, as the Saints lose Sam Flanders to a potentially serious Achilles injury.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL player is supported by two medical staff as he makes his way off the ground.

items.title: 'Unfair' Women's Origin timing criticised as Blues sweep Maroons aside

children.content: The captains of New South Wales and Queensland question the timing of the Women's State of Origin series after the Blues completed an historic clean sweep.

image.altText: Maroons players look sad after Women's State of Origin III.

items.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town

children.content: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.

items.title: Australian to join 'scary as hell' mission to rescue Laos villagers from cave

children.content: A diver describes the "emotional moment" when he found a group of men alive after they were trapped for more than a week in a Laos cave, while a South Australian renowned for his ability to navigate tight spaces joins the rescue mission.

image.altText: A man in a suit and orange helmet climbing backwards in a cave.

items.title: Government sues 3M Australia for $2 billion over PFAS contamination

children.content: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.

image.altText: Fire fighter sprays foam on a fire

items.title: 'Am I safe?' Return of 'ISIS brides' sparks fear in Yazidi communities

children.content: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.

gallery.altText: A portrait shot of Amera Ali.

thumbnail.altText: A portrait shot of Amera Ali.

items.title: Aussies facing 'most important day' of World Championship campaign

children.content: The threat of relegation hangs over the Australian men's rugby sevens squad ahead of the final two rounds of the World Championship series.

image.altText: Henry Hutchison of Australia runs the ball during a men's rugby sevens match in 2026.

items.title: Need for mine's hybrid power plant 'embarrassing', outback MP says

children.content: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.

image.altText: aerial shot of power plant under construction at a mine site in outback queensland

items.title: Qld government to push ahead with Victoria Park demolition amid heritage challenges

children.content: Excavators and fencing began appearing at the Brisbane park earlier this week, but the federal government has not yet assessed challenges under the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Protection Act.

gallery.altText: A woman in a blue jacket looking at a tree in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a blue jacket looking at a tree in the foreground.
